issue.author: shao-hua-li

comments.author: nikic
comments.body:
Based on opt-bisect it looks like the issue is introduced during `-loop-reduce` on:
```ll
; ModuleID = 'test.c'
source_filename = "test.c"
target datalayout = "e-m:e-p270:32:32-p271:32:32-p272:64:64-i64:64-f80:128-n8:16:32:64-S128"
target triple = "x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu"

@e = dso_local global i64 0, align 8
@g = dso_local local_unnamed_addr global ptr @e, align 8
@h = dso_local local_unnamed_addr global i32 0, align 4
@.str = private unnamed_addr constant [4 x i8] c"%d\0A\00", align 1

; Function Attrs: nofree nounwind
declare noundef i32 @printf(ptr nocapture noundef readonly, ...) local_unnamed_addr #3

define i64 @main() local_unnamed_addr #2 {
entry:
  br label %for.cond.i.i

for.cond.i.i:                                     ; preds = %for.cond.i.i, %entry 
  %c.0.i.i = phi i32 [ 1, %entry ], [ %inc.i.i, %for.cond.i.i ]
  %shl.i.i = shl i32 1, %c.0.i.i
  %cmp.not.i.i = icmp slt i32 %shl.i.i, -8
  %inc.i.i = add nuw nsw i32 %c.0.i.i, 1
  br i1 %cmp.not.i.i, label %for.cond.i.i, label %i.exit.i
  
i.exit.i:                                         ; preds = %for.cond.i.i
  %call1.i = tail call i32 (ptr, ...) @printf(ptr noundef nonnull @.str, i32 noundef %c.0.i.i)
  %add.i.i = sub nsw i32 83, %c.0.i.i
  %rem.i.i = srem i32 %add.i.i, 12
  %add1.i.i = sub nsw i32 9, %rem.i.i
  store i32 %add1.i.i, ptr @h, align 4
  %cmp.i.i = icmp ult i32 %add1.i.i, 32
  %tobool.not5.i.i = icmp ne i32 %rem.i.i, 9
  %tobool.not.i.i = and i1 %tobool.not5.i.i, %cmp.i.i
  br i1 %tobool.not.i.i, label %p.exit, label %if.then.i.i
  
if.then.i.i:                                      ; preds = %i.exit.i
  %sub2.i = sub nsw i32 0, %c.0.i.i
  %conv.i.i = sext i32 %sub2.i to i64
  store i64 %conv.i.i, ptr @e, align 8
  br label %p.exit
  
p.exit:                                           ; preds = %i.exit.i, %if.then.i.i
  %res = load i64, ptr @e, align 8
  ret i64 %res
}
```
Though alive does not report anything.

comments.author: nikic
comments.body: Somehow I had to go through two LSR runs, but this is the failing `-loop-reduce` transform: https://alive2.llvm.org/ce/z/TZhg9z

comments.author: bcl5980
comments.body:
It looks the regression point is 63f244c4f4f84fd066f
If we extend a negative const in SCEVAddExpr, it will be zext to a positive const. And if the add expr referenced by a SCEVUDivExpr, the error happen. 
For example in this case:
original i32 SCEV 
(12 * ({83,+,-1}<nuw><nsw><%loop> /u 12))<nuw><nsw>
will be extend to i64
(12 * ({83,+,4294967295}<nuw><nsw><%loop> /u 12))<nuw><nsw>

After add 4294967295, then div 12 will caused miscompile.

@timshen91

comments.author: fhahn
comments.body: Looks like this would be fixed by https://reviews.llvm.org/D153004

issue.title: LSR miscompile

tests.test_body:
target datalayout = "e-m:o-p270:32:32-p271:32:32-p272:64:64-i64:64-f80:128-n8:16:32:64-S128"
target triple = "x86_64-apple-macosx"

declare void @use(i64)

define i32 @test_pr62852() {
entry:
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %loop, %entry
  %iv.1 = phi i32 [ 1, %entry ], [ %dec.1, %loop ]
  %iv.2 = phi i64 [ 0, %entry ], [ %inc.1, %loop ]
  %inc.1 = add nsw i64 %iv.2, 1
  %dec.1 = add nsw i32 %iv.1, -1
  call void @use(i64 %iv.2)
  %cmp6.1 = icmp sgt i32 %iv.1, 0
  br i1 %cmp6.1, label %loop, label %exit

exit:                                             ; preds = %loop
  %iv.1.ext = zext i32 %iv.1 to i64
  call void @use(i64 %iv.1.ext)
  call void @use(i64 %iv.2)
  %rem = urem i32 %dec.1, 53
  ret i32 %rem
}

target datalayout = "e-m:o-p270:32:32-p271:32:32-p272:64:64-i64:64-f80:128-n8:16:32:64-S128"
target triple = "x86_64-apple-macosx"

declare void @use.i32(i32)

define i64 @test_pr58039() {
entry:
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %loop, %entry
  %iv = phi i64 [ 0, %entry ], [ %iv.next, %loop ]
  %iv1 = phi i32 [ -74, %entry ], [ %iv1.next, %loop ]
  %iv2 = phi i32 [ 83, %entry ], [ %iv2.next, %loop ]
  %iv3 = phi i32 [ 0, %entry ], [ %iv3.next, %loop ]
  call void @use.i32(i32 %iv3)
  %iv.next = add nuw nsw i64 %iv, 1
  %iv1.next = add nuw nsw i32 %iv1, 1
  %iv2.next = add nsw i32 %iv2, -1
  %iv3.next = add nuw nsw i32 %iv3, 1
  br i1 false, label %loop, label %exit

exit:                                             ; preds = %loop
  %i2 = udiv i32 %iv2.next, 12
  %i5 = mul nuw nsw i32 %i2, 12
  %i6 = add i32 %iv1, %i5
  %cmp3 = icmp ult i32 %i6, 32
  %spec.select = select i1 %cmp3, i64 0, i64 %iv.next
  ret i64 %spec.select
}

target datalayout = "e-m:o-p270:32:32-p271:32:32-p272:64:64-i64:64-f80:128-n8:16:32:64-S128"
target triple = "x86_64-apple-macosx"

declare void @use(i64)

define i32 @test_pr38847() {
entry:
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %loop, %entry
  %iv = phi i8 [ 1, %entry ], [ %iv.next, %loop ]
  %iv.next = add nsw i8 %iv, -1
  %ext = zext i8 %iv to i64
  call void @use(i64 %ext)
  %cmp2 = icmp sgt i8 %iv.next, -1
  br i1 %cmp2, label %loop, label %exit

exit:                                             ; preds = %loop
  %sext = sext i8 %iv.next to i32
  %rem = urem i32 %sext, 9
  ret i32 %rem
}
